data.title: KKR (KKR) Valuation Check As Flagship Americas Buyout Fund Nears Record Hard Cap
data.description:
KKR (KKR) is back in focus after reports that it is on track to exceed a $20b target for its Americas buyout fund, with investors committing enough capital to approach a record hard cap.

See our latest analysis for KKR.

Despite the fundraising headlines, KKR’s recent trading has been softer. The 7 day share price return is 7.74% and the 30 day share price return is 7.29% from the latest close of $121.25. The 1 year total shareholder return of 26.33% contrasts with a much stronger 3 year and 5 year total shareholder return of 123.57% and 225.30% respectively, suggesting longer term holders have seen substantial gains even as near term momentum has cooled.

If KKR’s latest fundraise has you thinking about where capital is flowing next, it could be a good moment to broaden your radar with fast growing stocks with high insider ownership.

With KKR’s shares softer over 1 year but still showing very strong 3 and 5 year total returns, and with fresh fundraising momentum in its flagship buyout fund, is today’s price a reset worth considering, or is the market already pricing in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 22.8% Undervalued

KKR's most followed narrative pegs fair value at $157.10 versus the last close of $121.25, which puts a spotlight on the earnings engine behind that gap.

Expansion of credit and asset-based finance platforms, with KKR now a leader in a $6 trillion+ market poised for further growth, provides a broader and more durable base of fee-related earnings while also increasing the potential for performance fees as these businesses scale. This diversification reduces earnings volatility and supports long-term earnings growth.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious what earnings path could back up that higher value, especially with revenue expected to trend lower while margins and profit growth move sharply the other way?

At the core of this narrative are two moving parts that pull in opposite directions: a modeled revenue decline over several years and a steep ramp in earnings powered by much higher margins and a richer fee mix. The fair value also leans on a future valuation multiple that remains above the broader capital markets group, along with assumptions about how quickly embedded gains can be turned into realized income, all filtered through a 9.31% discount rate.

Result: Fair Value of $157.10 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, that upside story depends on continued fundraising strength and stable credit conditions, and both could be tested if competition tightens or credit performance worries resurface.

Find out about the key risks to this KKR narrative.

Another Angle On Value

Those fair value models point to upside, but on plain P/E KKR looks expensive. The shares trade on 47.6x earnings versus 24.8x for the US Capital Markets group and a fair ratio of 26.1x. This suggests that a lot of good news is already in the price.

See what the numbers say about this price — find out in our valuation breakdown.

If you see the data differently or prefer to test your own assumptions, you can pull the numbers together and build a custom story in minutes: Do it your way.

A good starting point is our analysis highlighting 2 key rewards investors are optimistic about regarding KKR.

data.title: Is RH (RH) A Potential Opportunity After A 45% One-Year Share Price Decline
data.description:
• If you are trying to figure out whether RH at around US$228 per share looks like a bargain or a value trap, it helps to first step back and look at what the recent share price has been telling you.
  • The stock has seen mixed returns, with a 1.9% decline over the last 7 days, a 24.8% gain over the last 30 days, an 18.1% gain year to date, but also a 45.5% decline over 1 year, a 26.5% decline over 3 years and a 52.0% decline over 5 years.
  • These swings suggest sentiment around RH has shifted more than once, and recent news coverage has focused on how investors are reassessing the business model, capital allocation and growth ambitions. Headlines have tended to link the share price moves to changing expectations about how the brand positions itself in the higher end home furnishings market and how that could affect longer term returns.
  • Simply Wall St currently gives RH a valuation score of 2 out of 6. This means it screens as undervalued on 2 of the 6 checks used in that model. Next, we will walk through the usual valuation tools investors lean on, then finish with a different way of thinking about value that might give you a clearer overall picture.

RH scores just 2/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: RH Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model estimates what a business could be worth by projecting its future cash flows and then discounting those back to today’s dollars. It is essentially asking what RH’s future cash generation might be worth to you right now.

RH’s latest twelve month free cash flow is about $76.4 million. The current model uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, combining analyst estimates for the early years with longer term projections provided by Simply Wall St. For example, free cash flow is projected at $252.8 million in 2026 and $300.5 million in 2028, with further values extrapolated out to 2035. All these projected cash flows are discounted back to today using the DCF framework.

On this basis, the estimated intrinsic value comes out at about $404.21 per share, compared with a recent share price of roughly $228. That implies an intrinsic discount of around 43.5%, which indicates the shares screen as undervalued on this model.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests RH is undervalued by 43.5%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 864 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for RH.

Approach 2: RH Price vs Earnings

For a profitable business like RH, the P/E ratio is a common way to think about value because it links what you pay for the stock to the earnings the company is currently generating. The higher the expected growth and the lower the perceived risk, the more investors are usually willing to pay in terms of a higher P/E multiple.

RH currently trades on a P/E of 39.0x. That sits above both the Specialty Retail industry average P/E of 19.9x and a peer average of 18.4x, so on simple comparisons the shares look more expensive than many competitors.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for RH is 31.0x. This is a proprietary estimate of what a more “normal” P/E for the company could look like once you factor in its earnings growth profile, profit margins, risk characteristics, industry group and market cap together, rather than just lining it up against a crude sector average.

Because this Fair Ratio of 31.0x is below the current 39.0x P/E by a clear margin, RH screens as overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1428 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your RH Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives, a simple way for you to connect your view of RH’s story with the numbers behind it.

A Narrative is your own storyline for a company, where you spell out what you think happens to revenue, earnings and margins, then link that to a fair value per share instead of just accepting a single model output.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, Narratives are an accessible tool used by millions of investors. They help you turn a view like “RH can strengthen its brand and control costs” into a clear financial forecast and an estimated fair value that you can compare with today’s price to help you decide whether to buy, hold, or sell.

Narratives update automatically when new information such as earnings or news is added. For RH, that can mean one investor’s Narrative points to a fair value well below the current US$228 share price, while another investor’s Narrative supports a fair value well above it.

Do you think there's more to the story for RH? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.title: Record Loan Growth and Lower Charge‑offs Might Change The Case For Investing In Live Oak Bancshares (LOB)
data.description:
• Live Oak Bancshares reported past full-year 2025 results showing net interest income of US$448.36 million and net income of US$105.87 million, with earnings per share rising from the prior year as net charge-offs in the fourth quarter fell to US$13.74 million from US$33.57 million.
  • Management pointed to record loan production, strong deposit growth, and expanding small-dollar SBA lending and technology investments as key drivers of this improved performance and operational efficiency.
  • Next, we’ll examine how record loan production and deposit growth shape Live Oak Bancshares’ investment narrative in light of these results.

Uncover the next big thing with financially sound penny stocks that balance risk and reward.

Advertisement

What Is Live Oak Bancshares' Investment Narrative?

For Live Oak Bancshares, the core investment story still comes down to whether you believe in its digital-first small business banking model, supported by record loan production and strong deposit growth. The latest full-year 2025 results, including higher net interest income and net income, along with lower fourth quarter net charge-offs, reinforce that thesis rather than change it. Beating revenue and EPS expectations, plus the share price pop around TD Cowen’s positive initiation, suggest the market is starting to price in that momentum, so near-term catalysts now skew more toward continued loan and deposit growth and further efficiency gains from technology and AI investments. At the same time, credit quality and the bank’s relatively high level of bad loans remain front and center as key risks, even with the recent improvement in charge-offs.

However, investors should be aware that credit quality trends could look quite different if conditions shift. Live Oak Bancshares' shares have been on the rise but are still potentially undervalued by 29%. Find out what it's worth.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The Simply Wall St Community’s two fair value views, spanning roughly US$42 to about US$55.67, show how far apart individual expectations can be. Against that backdrop, the recent earnings beat and improving charge-offs give you more concrete data to weigh alongside these differing opinions when thinking about Live Oak Bancshares’ future performance.

data.title: Does Fusion Materials Lab Entry Recast Tiangong International’s Advanced Metallurgy Ambitions (SEHK:826)?
data.description:
• Tiangong International announced that its subsidiary Jiangsu Tiangong Aihe Technology was approved on 16 January 2026 to join the Institute of Energy, Hefei Comprehensive National Science Center’s joint laboratory for developing fusion-oriented advanced metallic materials, including large-scale powder metallurgy 304B7 high-boron steel and new neutron shield steel.
  • This collaboration gives Tiangong direct involvement in controlled nuclear fusion material research, potentially broadening applications for its powder metallurgy capabilities into higher-end technology fields.
  • We will look at how this fusion materials joint laboratory membership could reshape Tiangong International’s investment narrative around advanced powder metallurgy.

Trump has pledged to "unleash" American oil and gas and these 22 US stocks have developments that are poised to benefit.

Advertisement

What Is Tiangong International's Investment Narrative?

To own Tiangong International, you need to believe its core tooling and steel businesses can keep converting modest sales pressure into improving profitability, while management allocates capital carefully after recent equity issuance and dividend tweaks. The new fusion materials joint laboratory feels more like a long-dated options ticket than a near-term earnings driver, given the five-year horizon and research uncertainty, so it probably does not change the immediate catalysts around volume recovery, margin resilience and how the balance sheet is managed following the HK$78,207,500 loan. It does, however, slightly rebalance the story toward higher-end powder metallurgy, which could matter if utilization and pricing become more constrained. The biggest risk is that investors pay a relatively full multiple for growth and returns that remain modest if these R&D efforts underwhelm.

However, investors should also be aware of execution and payoff risks around the fusion R&D shift. Tiangong International's shares are on the way up, but could they be overextended? Uncover how much higher they are than fair value.

Exploring Other Perspectives

The single fair value estimate from the Simply Wall St Community clusters around HK$0.10, which is far below recent trading levels. Some community members clearly see substantial downside risk, while others may focus on Tiangong’s fusion materials partnership and core margin trends as potential offsets, underscoring how differently people can interpret the same mix of R&D uncertainty and earnings momentum.

Explore another fair value estimate on Tiangong International - why the stock might be worth less than half the current price!

data.title: Is Brookdale Senior Living (BKD) Pricing Fully Reflecting Its Strong Multi Year Share Price Run?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Brookdale Senior Living's share price still offers value after a strong run, this article walks through what the current market price might be implying about the company.
  • The stock last closed at US$12.95, with returns of 6.1% over 7 days, 18.9% over 30 days, 19.5% year to date, 163.2% over 1 year, 339.0% over 3 years, and 162.1% over 5 years, which has put it firmly on many investors' radar.
  • Recent coverage around Brookdale Senior Living has focused on its position within the US senior living and healthcare space, including how it is responding to demand for assisted living and related services. These themes have helped frame investor expectations and provide context for the share price moves you have seen.
  • Right now Brookdale Senior Living scores 3 out of 6 on our valuation checks, giving it a valuation score of 3. Next we will look at what traditional valuation approaches say about that number, before finishing with a more complete way to think about the company's value.

Brookdale Senior Living delivered 163.2% returns over the last year. See how this stacks up to the rest of the Healthcare industry.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Brookdale Senior Living Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today’s value. This gives an estimate of what the whole business might be worth per share.

For Brookdale Senior Living, the model used is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, based on cash flow projections in $. The latest twelve month free cash flow is a loss of $133.3 million. Analysts provide explicit forecasts out to 2027, with free cash flow of $114.8 million in that year. Beyond that, Simply Wall St extrapolates cash flows, with ten year projections rising to $343.7 million in 2035, all adjusted back to today using a discount rate.

Putting those discounted cash flows together gives an estimated intrinsic value of $18.59 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $12.95, the model implies an intrinsic discount of 30.3%, which indicates the shares are trading below this cash flow based estimate.

Result: UNDERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Brookdale Senior Living is undervalued by 30.3%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 864 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Brookdale Senior Living.

Approach 2: Brookdale Senior Living Price vs Sales

For companies where earnings and book value are less reliable guides, price to sales, or P/S, can be a useful cross check because revenue tends to be more stable and less affected by accounting choices. Investors often look for a P/S level that lines up with the company’s growth profile and risk, with higher growth and lower perceived risk usually justifying a higher multiple, and slower growth or higher risk pointing to a lower one.

Brookdale Senior Living currently trades on a P/S of 1.00x. That sits close to both the peer group average of 0.95x and the wider Healthcare industry average of 1.31x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Brookdale Senior Living is 0.67x, which is its view of what a “normal” P/S might look like once you factor in items such as earnings growth, profit margins, industry, company size and specific risks.

This Fair Ratio is designed to be a more tailored anchor than a simple peer or industry comparison because it adjusts for those company specific characteristics rather than assuming all Healthcare stocks deserve similar multiples. With the actual P/S of 1.00x sitting above the Fair Ratio of 0.67x, the shares screen as overvalued on this metric.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/S ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1428 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Brookdale Senior Living Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to think about valuation, and that is through Narratives. These let you attach a clear story, your view on Brookdale Senior Living’s future revenue, earnings and margins, to the numbers behind your fair value estimate.

A Narrative is simply your explanation of what you think is happening at a company, linked to a forecast and then to a fair value. Instead of just seeing a single number, you see the beliefs and assumptions that produce it.

On Simply Wall St’s Community page, used by millions of investors, Narratives are presented as an easy tool where you can compare your own fair value for Brookdale Senior Living with other investors and decide whether the current price around US$12.95 looks high or low relative to those stories.

Because Narratives are refreshed when new information appears, such as news or earnings, your fair value view can update in step. This can help you judge whether the gap between Fair Value and Price is widening or closing, and therefore whether you might want to buy, hold or sell.

For example, one Brookdale Senior Living Narrative might assume a much higher fair value based on stronger long term demand for senior living services, while another might set a lower fair value due to concerns about costs and execution risk.

data.title: Assessing Gevo (GEVO) Valuation After Major Carbon Credit Milestone And Leadership Shift
data.description:
Gevo (GEVO) is in focus after its North Dakota carbon capture facility issued more than 500,000 engineered carbon removal certificates, received an A rating from BeZero Carbon, and appointed Alex Clayton as Chief Carbon Officer.

See our latest analysis for Gevo.

Gevo’s recent carbon removal milestone and leadership changes have arrived alongside a 6.3% 1 day share price return and an 8.96% 7 day share price return. However, the 90 day share price return of an 11.34% decline suggests momentum has been uneven despite an 18.06% 1 year total shareholder return.

If this kind of low carbon fuels story has your attention, it could be a good moment to scan other high growth tech and AI stocks for fresh ideas in the broader energy transition theme.

With Gevo shares at US$2.19, trading at what screens as a low value score of 2 and a large discount to both analyst targets and intrinsic estimates, you have to ask: is this a mispriced decarbonization play, or is the market already baking in future growth?

Advertisement

Most Popular Narrative: 64% Undervalued

Simply Wall St's most followed narrative pegs Gevo's fair value at $6.08, a large gap to the recent $2.19 close that raises clear questions about what is baked into those assumptions.

Monetization and growth of high integrity carbon dioxide removal (CDR) credits and clean fuel production tax credits (CFPCs), enabled by Gevo's uniquely certified carbon sequestration site, provide new stable, high margin revenue streams and cost offsets that are expected to meaningfully lift recurring net income and reduce volatility.

Read the complete narrative.

Curious how that carbon credit engine feeds into the $6.08 fair value, using a 6.96% discount rate and ambitious growth and margin steps that the market is clearly debating.

Result: Fair Value of $6.08 (UNDERVALUED)

Have a read of the narrative in full and understand what's behind the forecasts.

However, there are still clear watchpoints, including Gevo's reliance on government tax credits, as well as the heavy capital needs and execution risk around new fuel projects.

Find out about the key risks to this Gevo narrative.

Another View: Price To Sales Paints A Tougher Picture

That 64% undervaluation signal sits awkwardly next to Gevo's current P/S of 4.3x, compared with 1.6x for the US Oil and Gas industry and 0.9x for peers and the fair ratio. If the market leans toward those lower anchors instead, the perceived discount could be far slimmer than it looks today.

data.title: Will Keppel’s (SGX:BN4) 1 GW Asia-Pacific Data Centre Push Reshape Its Infrastructure Narrative?
data.description:
• Keppel Ltd. has expanded its data centre powerbank pipeline in Asia Pacific to over 1.0 GW of gross power capacity, including securing rights to lease a 123-hectare site near Morwell in Victoria, Australia, for up to 720 MW in phases.
  • This Australian site, located within the proposed Gippsland Renewable Energy Zone and linked to major power and fibre infrastructure, could enhance Keppel’s ability to offer lower-cost, high-connectivity data centre solutions while supporting growth in its funds under management.
  • We’ll now examine how this large-scale data centre powerbank expansion, particularly the Morwell development, shapes Keppel’s broader investment narrative.

The end of cancer? These 29 emerging AI stocks are developing tech that will allow early identification of life changing diseases like cancer and Alzheimer's.

Advertisement

What Is Keppel's Investment Narrative?

To own Keppel today, you really have to believe in its pivot from a traditional industrial conglomerate into a scaled, fee-earning digital infrastructure and asset management platform. The latest data centre powerbank move, especially the Morwell site, reinforces that story by expanding its addressable pipeline without putting big capex on Keppel’s own balance sheet in the near term. In the short run, though, this announcement is unlikely to move the needle on upcoming earnings releases, especially given management’s guidance that it should not materially affect current-year EPS or NTA. Instead, it subtly reshapes the catalyst and risk mix: execution on Morwell and filling that 1.0 GW pipeline now matter more, while existing concerns around premium valuation, one off gains in recent results and weaker cash flow coverage of dividends remain front of mind.

However, one key operational risk in scaling these data centres is easy to overlook, and investors should not. Keppel's shares are on the way up, but they could be overextended by 24%. Uncover the fair value now.

Exploring Other Perspectives

Four fair value estimates from the Simply Wall St Community span about S$8.06 to S$11.26 per share, showing how differently individual investors see Keppel. Set that against the growing importance of data centre execution risk after the Morwell announcement, and you can see why it may pay to compare a few of these viewpoints before deciding how resilient the business really is.

Explore 4 other fair value estimates on Keppel - why the stock might be worth as much as SGD11.26!

data.title: Is It Too Late To Consider Costco Wholesale (COST) After Strong Five Year Share Price Gains?
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Costco Wholesale's share price still makes sense at current levels, you are not alone. Many investors are asking what they are actually paying for at this point.
  • The stock recently closed at US$983.25, with returns of 2.0% over 7 days, 12.8% over 30 days, 15.1% year to date, 5.2% over 1 year, very large gains over 3 years and 194.9% over 5 years.
  • Recent coverage has focused on Costco's position as a membership based retailer and its role in everyday consumer spending. This helps frame how investors think about its share price at these levels. Commentary has also highlighted how its warehouse model and membership economics are central to the investment debate, especially when the stock is trading near record territory.
  • Despite that backdrop, Costco Wholesale currently scores 0 out of 6 on our valuation checks. Next we will walk through traditional valuation methods and then finish with a more complete way of thinking about what this business might be worth.

Costco Wholesale scores just 0/6 on our valuation checks. See what other red flags we found in the full valuation breakdown.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Costco Wholesale Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow model takes estimates of a company’s future cash flows and discounts them back to today using a required rate of return, to arrive at an estimate of what the business might be worth per share.

For Costco Wholesale, the model used here is a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach. The latest twelve month Free Cash Flow is about $9.4b. Analysts provide explicit Free Cash Flow estimates for several years, and Simply Wall St then extrapolates further, with projections in the data set running out to 2035. In that year, forecast Free Cash Flow is $18.4b, discounted to $9.4b in today’s terms.

Aggregating these discounted cash flows produces an estimated intrinsic value of about $790.29 per share. Compared with the recent share price of $983.25, the DCF output suggests Costco Wholesale trades at roughly a 24.4% premium to this estimate. On this model the stock appears overvalued.

Result: OVERVALUED

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Costco Wholesale may be overvalued by 24.4%. Discover 864 undervalued stocks or create your own screener to find better value opportunities.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Costco Wholesale.

Approach 2: Costco Wholesale Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company, the P/E ratio is a straightforward way to think about what you are paying for each dollar of current earnings. It connects directly to the business’s ability to generate profit, which is ultimately what supports a share price over time.

What counts as a "normal" or "fair" P/E depends on how the market views a company’s growth outlook and risk. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk are often associated with a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher uncertainty usually align with a lower P/E.

Costco Wholesale currently trades on a P/E of 52.57x. That is well above the Consumer Retailing industry average of 22.17x and above the peer group average of 25.58x. Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio for Costco Wholesale is 36.20x, which is its proprietary view of what a justified P/E might be after considering factors like earnings growth, profit margins, industry, market cap and risk profile.

This Fair Ratio aims to be more tailored than a simple comparison with industry or peers because it incorporates company specific characteristics rather than broad group averages. Compared with this Fair Ratio, Costco Wholesale’s current 52.57x P/E looks elevated, suggesting the shares are pricing in more than what this framework would imply.

Result: OVERVALUED

P/E ratios tell one story, but what if the real opportunity lies elsewhere? Discover 1428 companies where insiders are betting big on explosive growth.

Upgrade Your Decision Making: Choose your Costco Wholesale Narrative

Earlier we mentioned that there is an even better way to understand valuation, so let us introduce you to Narratives. These are simply your story about Costco Wholesale, captured in numbers like your assumed fair value and your own estimates for future revenue, earnings and margins.

A Narrative connects three pieces in a straight line: what you think is happening at the company, the forecast that flows from that view, and the fair value that falls out of those assumptions so you can compare it directly with today’s share price.

On Simply Wall St, Narratives sit inside the Community page and are designed to be easy to use so you can quickly see how your view on Costco Wholesale stacks up against others, and decide whether the gap between Fair Value and Price suggests you might want to buy, hold, or sell.

Narratives also refresh automatically when new information arrives, for example earnings releases or news. You might see one Costco Wholesale Narrative that assumes a much higher fair value and stronger outlook and another that builds in more conservative margins and a lower fair value, all based on the same current share price.

Do you think there's more to the story for Costco Wholesale? Head over to our Community to see what others are saying!

data.description:

What Is ATOSS Software's Investment Narrative?

For ATOSS, the big picture you need to buy into is a premium-priced, profitable software business where quality and consistency matter more than rapid expansion. Revenue and earnings have been growing, but at a moderating pace, and the stock already trades on a higher multiple than peers, which makes execution the key short term catalyst. Upcoming results and guidance, particularly around the 2025 revenue and margin targets, remain in focus. The fresh Jefferies and Deutsche Bank endorsements, alongside the CFO’s investor call, directly address one of the softer points in the story: communication and visibility. If that call convincingly links current performance, dividend policy and long term ambitions, it could support sentiment, but the recent mixed returns suggest the valuation risk is still front and center.

data.title: How Investors Are Reacting To Altus Group (TSX:AIF) Landing Cushman & Wakefield ARGUS Rollout
data.description:
• Altus Group Limited recently announced that Cushman & Wakefield has chosen its ARGUS Intelligence platform, along with Portfolio Manager, for a global rollout to support valuation and portfolio performance analysis across the firm’s commercial real estate operations.
  • This global deployment by a major industry player underscores how Altus’ analytics and benchmarking tools are becoming more embedded in institutional real estate decision‑making and workflow transparency.
  • We’ll now examine how this global ARGUS Intelligence adoption shapes Altus Group’s investment narrative, particularly around its role in commercial real estate analytics.

Find companies with promising cash flow potential yet trading below their fair value.

Advertisement

What Is Altus Group's Investment Narrative?

For Altus Group, the core investment story is about believing in its transition from a traditional real estate services business toward a data and software‑led model, even as earnings remain under pressure and the valuation looks rich on standard metrics. The newly announced global rollout of ARGUS Intelligence and Portfolio Manager at Cushman & Wakefield supports that narrative by reinforcing Altus’ relevance in institutional commercial real estate workflows, but, on its own, it may not dramatically change near‑term financial catalysts like muted revenue guidance, low Return on Equity and volatile reported earnings affected by large one‑off items. It does, however, slightly rebalance the risk profile: execution in software, client adoption and retention look incrementally better anchored, while the share price weakness and sizeable buyback capacity keep capital allocation decisions firmly in focus for existing and prospective shareholders.

However, one key operational risk could still catch some investors off guard. Despite retreating, Altus Group's shares might still be trading 20% above their fair value. Discover the potential downside here.

data.title: Assessing Group 1 Automotive (GPI) Valuation As Fresh Analyst Buy Ratings Highlight Earnings Potential
data.description:
Recent analyst actions, including a new buy initiation from Evercore and positive earnings expectations, have brought Group 1 Automotive (GPI) into focus for investors monitoring sentiment shifts ahead of the next results.

See our latest analysis for Group 1 Automotive.

At a share price of US$391.91, Group 1 Automotive has seen a 90 day share price return of 7.56% decline and a 1 year total shareholder return of 12.42% decline, although the 5 year total shareholder return of 194.50% points to stronger longer term momentum than recent trading suggests.

If the recent analyst attention has you reassessing the auto space, it could be a good moment to scan other auto manufacturers that might fit your watchlist next.

With analysts highlighting potential upside to their price targets and the share price recovering from recent declines, the key question now is whether Group 1 Automotive is still trading at a discount or if the market has already priced in future growth.

Most Popular Narrative: 15% Undervalued

Against a fair value narrative of $460.88, Group 1 Automotive's last close at $391.91 reflects a valuation gap that this widely followed view seeks to explain.

The sustained growth in the high margin parts & service (aftersales) segment, driven by an aging vehicle fleet and rising average vehicle age in both the U.S. and U.K., positions Group 1 to capitalize on increasing repair and maintenance needs, which should continue to expand recurring revenue and bolster margins.

Ongoing expansion of technician headcount, investments in service capacity, and focus on customer outreach to owners of older vehicles are set to further increase aftersales throughput, providing earnings stability and margin growth that are less correlated to vehicle sales cycles.

However, this depends on Group 1 keeping acquisition risks in check and avoiding margin pressure if online retailers and direct to consumer models gain more ground.

Another View: Market Ratios Paint A Different Picture

While the fair value narrative points to Group 1 Automotive trading at a discount, the market is sending a mixed signal. The current P/E of 13.1x is slightly higher than the peer average of 12.7x, yet sits well below both the US Specialty Retail industry at 20.5x and the fair ratio of 17.5x, which suggests the multiple could shift if sentiment changes.

That gap cuts both ways, as it hints at some valuation risk relative to close peers but also leaves room if the market edges closer to the fair ratio. Which reference point do you think matters more for your own thesis on GPI?
